findings.description: The proposed context_selectors.py mixes file-range parsing, git resolution, diff collection, and repomix capture, violating concern boundaries and creating a high-drift 'god helper'.
findings.fix: Split functionality into discrete modules such as file_specs.py, git_context.py, and repomix_source.py.
findings.title: God-module risk in context_selectors.py

findings.description: The plan only migrates prompt/context assembly, leaving config parsing and include-pattern construction in shell scripts (generate-overview.sh/batch.sh), resulting in partial unification.
findings.fix: Centralize overview config parsing and repomix argument construction in Python.
findings.title: Incomplete overview migration allows continued drift

findings.description: The BuildArtifact schema lacks the prompt string data ('Write the requested codebase overview...') required by the overview flow, making the handoff boundary insufficient.
findings.fix: Extend BuildArtifact to include prompt payloads or ensure the shared core handles task-specific instructions.
findings.title: BuildArtifact underspecified for overview requirements

findings.description: Plan distinguishes hashes but fails to define newline normalization, trailing whitespace, path normalization, or section ordering, making hashes sensitive to environment/OS.
findings.fix: Specify and implement a normalization contract including versioning and ordering rules before hashing.
findings.title: Deterministic hashing lacks normalization contract

findings.description: System uses chars for some truncations, tokens for others, and 'bytes/4' for estimates; a shared budget engine cannot function without a shared metric.
findings.fix: Adopt a single shared metric engine and label manifests with the estimation method used.
findings.title: Unification fails due to inconsistent budget metrics

findings.description: The current plan still allows model-review to write N identical context files for N axes, maintaining duplication of content and storage.
findings.fix: Update model-review to point all axes to a single shared content path if the content hash is identical.
findings.title: Model-review creates redundant context files

findings.description: Using grep to prevent helper duplication is easily evaded by renaming and doesn't verify behavioral consolidation.
findings.fix: Replace grep checks with behavioral contract tests and strict import constraints in CI.
findings.title: Grep-based enforcement is architecturally weak

findings.description: Current strategy lacks explicit handling for binary files, symlinks, or submodules, which may lead to silent mis-rendering.
findings.fix: Implement excerpt policy tests specifically for binary, symlink, and submodule inputs.
findings.title: Missing policy for non-text files
